name: Clay Pot Shrimp with Bean Thread Noodles- Go
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Whole White or Black Peppercorns
2 tbsp Coarsely Chopped Fresh Cilantro Roots or Leaves and Stems
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the Cilantro Pesto: Using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder, crush or grind the peppercorns to a fine powder. Combine the pepper, cilantro roots, and garlic; work the ingredients into a fairly smooth paste in the mortar or a small blender or food processor. If using a blender or food processor, add a little vegetable oil or water to ease grinding. Make about 1/4 cup. For an industrial strength batch use 1 tbsp peppercorns, 1/2 cup cilantro roots and 1/2 cup garlic. Store the pesto in a glass jar, pour a little oil on top to cover, and seal tightly. It will keep nicely for about 1 week in the refrigerator.
2. Prepare the Shrimp: Place the noodles in a large bowl and add warm water to cover. Soak the noodles until they become limp and white, about 15 minutes. Drain and set aside. You should have about 5 cups softened noodles. In a large clay pot or flameproof, heavy-bottomed casserole, heat the oil over medium heat until hot, about 1 minute. Add the pesto and stir-fry until fragrant, about 1 minute, adding a little more oil if it sticks or burns. Add the ginger and shrimp and stir-fry for 1 minute. Toss in the green onion, turning the mixture once more. Transfer the shrimp to a plate and set aside while you prepare the noodles and sauce.
3. Prepare the Sauce: In a small bowl, combine the chicken stock, fish sauce (Nam Pla), oyster sauce, rice wine or dry sherry, soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and salt; stir to mix well.
4. Cook the Noodles: Place the soaked noodles in the clay pot in which you cooked the shrimp mixture. Scrape the shrimp mixture over the noodles and pour in the chicken stock mixture. Toss the noodles and shrimp a little to combine them with the sauce, then cover the pot tightly. Place the clay pot over medium heat and cook until the noodles are soft and clear and the shrimp is done, about 10 minutes.
5. Serve immediately, sprinkling with cilantro leaves.
